name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. Q1 2026 subscription revenue reached $3.67B (+22% YoY), with full-year 2026 guidance raised to $15.74-15.78B. The company's Now Assist AI suite had net new ACV more than double YoY, with 244 transactions exceeding $1M in net new ACV. CEO Bill McDermott made a $3M open-market share purchase in February 2026 and extended his contract to 2030. ServiceNow serves 8,100+ customers including 85% of the Fortune 500.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has fundamentally transformed from an ITSM ticketing system to an enterprise AI platform — a successful business model pivot that expanded TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ [1]. The platform now orchestrates AI agents across enterprise workflows, and the company launched a real-time data foundation for autonomous AI and opened its full system of action to every AI agent in the enterprise in 2026 [3]. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist customers spending over $1M in ACV grew over 130% YoY, with net new ACV more than doubling YoY. 244 transactions exceeded $1M in net new ACV, nearly 40% growth [1]. 16 transactions over $5M in net new ACV in Q1 2026, representing 80% YoY growth. These are shipping AI products generating measurable, accelerating revenue. Market Share (2/2): Category-defining leader in enterprise workflow orchestration with 85% of Fortune 500 as customers, 630 customers with $5M+ ACV (+22% YoY), and no close independent competitor [1]. The Accenture Forward Deployed Engineering partnership, Google Cloud Gemini integration, and NVIDIA AI agent partnership further cement platform dominance [3]. Pipeline (1/2): Armis cybersecurity acquisition expected to close 2026, adding security operations TAM. Full-year guidance raised $205M to $15.74-15.78B. However, specific next-gen product launch dates beyond current AI agent capabilities are less defined. R&D Intensity (1/1): Gross margins of 76.6% support substantial R&D investment above the software industry average [1]. Total: 3+2+2+1+1 = 9.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's mission — to 'make the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, thoughtful, and connected to a genuine societal benefit: removing friction from the workplace to free humans for higher-value work [1]. The purpose is validated by consistent platform expansion and customer retention (NRR >125% sustained for years). It differentiates from peers and is clearly referenced in strategic decisions. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits, and the company has committed to carbon neutrality. Genuine but not distinctive community programs [1]. Employee Retention (1/2): 29,187 employees with above-average Glassdoor ratings for enterprise software. The company's 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creates psychological safety. Low voluntary attrition relative to tech sector peers [1]. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO Bill McDermott purchased 28,677 shares at $104.60/share on February 27, 2026, for $3M in open-market buying [4]. Additionally, McDermott and other senior leaders terminated their Rule 10b5-1 selling plans, cancelling all future pre-planned sales. This is an exceptionally strong insider signal — not only buying $3M personally but stopping all pre-planned selling. Contract extended to 2030. Total: 3+1+1+2 = 7.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Growth stock scoring. Liquidity check: $112.45 x 26,500,082 avg daily volume = ~$2.98B — PASSES. Q1 2026 subscription revenue $3.67B (+22% YoY), total revenue $3.77B (+22% YoY), cRPO $12.64B (+22.5% YoY), RPO $27.7B (+25% YoY) [1]. Full-year guidance raised to $15.74-15.78B (+20.5-21% YoY). D/E 20.7%: (300-20.7)/(300-70)=1.0 capped. ROE 16.07%: 1.0 (2x weight). Rev growth YoY 22.1%: 1.0. Rev 5yr CAGR 23.0%: 1.0 (2x weight). Forward P/E 22.37: (60-22.37)/(60-20)=0.941. Profit margin 12.59%: (12.59-0)/(20-0)=0.629. Forward PEG 1.16: (3-1.16)/(3-1)=0.920 (2x weight). Weighted sum: 1.0+2.0+1.0+2.0+0.941+0.629+1.840 = 9.410. Max weighted sum: 10. Score: 9.41/10*10 = 9.41, rounded to 9.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): ServiceNow is not threatened by AI — it IS AI infrastructure for the enterprise. The company's thesis, validated by Q1 2026 results, is that AI agents need a system of action to execute decisions across legacy enterprise environments, and ServiceNow controls that execution layer [1][3]. CEO McDermott's argument — 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions' — is directly validated by the Q1 data: Now Assist ACV more than doubled YoY, 244 AI transactions exceeded $1M, and the company opened its full system of action to every AI agent in the enterprise [1][3]. JPMorgan and multiple analysts explicitly identify ServiceNow as an 'AI-resilient' software platform. Partnerships with NVIDIA (autonomous AI agents), Google Cloud (Gemini integration), and Accenture (Forward Deployed Engineering for agentic AI at scale) position ServiceNow as the neutral orchestration layer for multi-vendor AI deployments. The only residual risk is a theoretical scenario where agentic AI systems mature to entirely replace workflow orchestration platforms — a risk that diminishes with each quarter of deepening enterprise integration. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist AI suite generating quantifiable, rapidly growing revenue with ACV doubling YoY [1]. Total: 8+1 = 9.

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott is a rare enterprise software CEO who operates explicitly with long-term platform thinking. His $3M personal share purchase during the February 2026 software sector correction, cancellation of all pre-planned stock sales, and contract extension to 2030 reflect maximum personal conviction in the Just Cause. ServiceNow's transformation from ITSM ticketing to enterprise AI platform, executed over 20 years with expanding TAM at each step, is textbook infinite game strategy. The partnerships with NVIDIA, Google Cloud, and Accenture for agentic AI at scale demonstrate that the platform is becoming the indispensable neutral layer for enterprise AI execution.

just_cause.analysis: ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuinely transformative and increasingly validated. The platform has evolved from ITSM to enterprise workflow to AI orchestration, with each evolution driven by the same cause: remove friction so humans can focus on higher-value work [1]. McDermott's consistent articulation across multiple economic cycles, his $3M personal share purchase, and his 2030 contract extension validate that the cause shapes real strategic decisions. The 85% Fortune 500 penetration and 22% subscription growth demonstrate the cause generates competitive advantage. Score: 3 points (Just Cause is the competitive advantage), display 10.

trusting_teams.analysis: ServiceNow's 29,187 employees operate in a culture that consistently ranks in the top tier for enterprise software [1]. The company's investment in employee AI training and its 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creates psychological safety at a moment when most workers fear AI displacement. CEO approval ratings above 90%. The Forward Deployed Engineering partnership with Accenture explicitly co-locates ServiceNow and Accenture teams, reflecting high-trust collaboration [3]. Score: 2 points (high-trust organization), display 10.

worthy_rivals.analysis: McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose platform capabilities push ServiceNow to expand [1]. The NVIDIA partnership and Google Cloud integration reflect competitive awareness — rather than building AI models independently, ServiceNow embraces multi-vendor AI to strengthen its orchestration layer [3]. Armis acquisition addresses a security operations gap revealed by competitive dynamics. Score: 1 point, display 10.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise workflow platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility — each transformation built on the previous while expanding TAM 10x [1]. In 2026, opening the full system of action to every AI agent in the enterprise and launching a real-time data foundation for autonomous AI represents another significant capability expansion driven by the Just Cause, not market pressure [3]. Score: 2 points (transformative flexibility, multiple successful pivots), display 10.

courage_to_lead.analysis: McDermott's $3M personal share purchase at $104.60 on February 27, 2026 — during a period when the stock had fallen from $211 to $105 (a 50% decline) amid AI disruption fears — is precisely Sinek's definition of courage: acting in service of the Just Cause at significant personal financial cost [4]. He simultaneously terminated all pre-planned selling and extended his contract to 2030. The Rule of 55+ standard (revenue growth + FCF margin must exceed 55) maintains long-term capital discipline. These are not gestures — they are pattern-forming acts of financial conviction. Score: 2 points (pattern of courage), display 10.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the premier enterprise AI platform, and Q1 2026 confirmed the thesis with accelerating metrics: subscription revenue $3.67B (+22% YoY), cRPO $12.64B (+22.5% YoY), RPO $27.7B (+25% YoY), and full-year guidance raised $205M to $15.74-15.78B [1]. The AI story is not hypothetical — Now Assist customers spending $1M+ in ACV grew 130% YoY, net new AI ACV more than doubled, and 244 AI transactions exceeded $1M [1]. CEO McDermott's $3M personal share purchase at $104.60, cancellation of all selling plans, and 2030 contract extension represent maximum insider conviction [4]. Strategic partnerships with NVIDIA (autonomous AI agents), Google Cloud (Gemini integration), and Accenture (Forward Deployed Engineering at scale) position ServiceNow as the neutral AI orchestration layer for the enterprise — the company AI agents must execute through [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case is valuation compression risk: at 22.4x forward earnings and ~7.5x forward revenue, ServiceNow prices in sustained 20%+ growth for years. The stock has fallen 47% from its 52-week high of $211 to $112, demonstrating that growth premium stocks can de-rate violently even with strong fundamentals [5]. If agentic AI matures faster than the 'execution layer' thesis anticipates, enterprise workflows could theoretically be replaced rather than orchestrated. EPS growth decelerated to just 2.3% YoY in Q1 2026 despite strong revenue growth, reflecting investment spending that compresses near-term margins. Profit margin of 12.59% is below the 20% target for infrastructure scoring. The Armis acquisition adds integration complexity to an already ambitious AI platform expansion.
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI ACV tripling in FY2026, with AI-attached deal sizes expanding as enterprises move from pilot to platform-wide agentic AI deployment across IT, HR, and customer service
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing in H2 2026, adding ~$400M in security operations revenue and expanding the ServiceNow platform TAM into a $30B+ adjacent market
Full-year 2026 subscription revenue guidance of $15.74-15.78B representing 20.5-21% growth, with potential for further raise as AI agent adoption accelerates in H2
key_risks: Valuation fragility: stock has already fallen 47% from $211 high despite strong fundamentals — any growth deceleration below 20% could trigger further multiple compression from current 22x forward P/E
Margin compression: profit margin of 12.59% and EPS growth of only 2.3% YoY in Q1 2026 suggest heavy investment spending that could disappoint profitability-focused investors

Agentic AI risk: if autonomous AI systems mature to execute enterprise workflows without an orchestration layer, the 'ServiceNow as AI control tower' thesis would be challenged — a low-probability but high-impact scenario
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Financial Results — $3.67B subscription revenue, 22% growth, guidance raised $205M, SEC/ServiceNow Newsroom, April 2026
ServiceNow 2026 Proxy Statement — DEF 14A, SEC, April 2026
ServiceNow AI Agent Partnerships — NVIDIA, Google Cloud, Accenture Forward Deployed Engineering, ServiceNow Newsroom, 2026
ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott $3M Share Purchase — 28,677 shares at $104.60, Motley Fool/StockTitan/Glekko, February 27, 2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW key statistics, accessed June 2026
